title: Level 257
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=June 27, 2026|1=For being uploaded in 2025, this is very reminiscent of 2022. A bit of modernizing could go well here (especially in removing the unnecessary concepts, like the pills)}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Scene 75; they’re still alive somewhere out there…}}__NOTOC__{{Cleantitle}}
{{Class Variable}}
{{Blurimage|file=Sw.jpg|caption=An image of Level 257.}}
{{Background|file=Sw.jpg}}
==Description==
Level 257 is an indoor inflatable park, each room comprising trampolines, slides, ball pits, etc. The rooms are 100x50x100 meters, interconnected via a metallic door placed randomly in the room. Each room is superficial; the neighbouring room typically containing little difference. Doors have a chance to be replaced with a ‘false exit’, consisting of hallways which lead to a dead-end. These hallways are known to be heavily non-Euclidean, shifts being extremely severe, resulting in one being suffocated in a wall, or no clipped into [[The Void]]. The hallway leads into a darker version of the level.

The lights emit mild gamma radiation, standing directly under the lights  even for only 20 minutes is able to pose risk to radiation sickness. Analysis of the lights shows that they run on no power sources, with there being no wires in addition. During [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], the lights did not go out but the radiation disappeared reportedly.

All areas of the level boasts mild non-commutative geometry, which gets more severe in the dark areas of the level. Non-commutative geometry refers to the order in which certain things matter, as they change the outcome. In this context, the destination. For instance, you wanted to enter a slide; there are 2 ways, A and B. And in A and B, there are other ways. Let’s call them C and D. If you took A then C, you would end up on the other side of the room. If you took B then D, you would end up in A.

Hallucinations on this level are known to be strong; many reports perceiving the environment as “zigzags”. Akin to drug effects, some also report LSD-like hallucinations. Aftereffects can include fatigue, nausea, and alertness. Notably, a long-term aftereffect are more noticeable afterimages, even in dark rooms. Additionally, one can not alleviate this with [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. Instead, finding the yellow and green pill (see the 'Objects' section) or bringing [[Object 41|Hydricpoxic Oil]] is considered one of the only ways to diminish these hallucinations and to maintain your sanity.

Breaking stuff will result in a new room forming, and a new door forming. Breaking the walls will result in the broken part becoming the door to either a hallway or a room. It is not recommended to step into these new rooms, as since [[The Void]] encompasses the entirety of the level, and the floor of the new room is generally unstable, one could fall into the Void. One should wait 1 day before stepping into the new room. In these new rooms, resources can be found, such as variants of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], and pills. A table that documents each pill and its effects can be present in the objects section.

The level kills almost all organic life, except humans, some bacteria and viruses, and rarely, entities (it should be noted that certain entities and level-exclusive entities are immune). Influenza and paramyxovirus have been known to plague the outskirts of the level, usually staying there until they sporadically infect spawn momentarily during June to August.

Overtime, unattended clean water can gradually turn into [[Object 1|Almond Water]] within 8 to 9 months. Although, if left still for too long, there’s a chance it may get infected with the level’s viruses, get absorbed into the level, or even turn into [[Object 1|Liquid Pain]]. However, if you leave untransformed [[Object 1|Almond Water]] for the same amount of time, it transforms into [[Object 44|Cashew Water]] instead.

One can find it hard to sleep if one stays at this level for at least 1 month, many generally get only 1 to 4 hours of sleep or end up getting insomnia. Studies have shown neurotransmitters, specifically norepinephrine, responsible for waking up the body, are transmitting randomly, starting from when you enter this level, gradually activating the transmitter more and more. Similarly, dopamine, over time gradually becomes more inactive, 5 months of staying at this level makes the transmitter unable to function. However, if one leaves the level, these transmitters will heal slowly (taking years at times).

Randomly, one can become temporarily fully blind, or in rare cases, become color blind. It can last from 5 minutes to 2 hours. Recent research has shown that a heavy amount of stress can make this more likely to happen, the same with anxiety. Aftereffects can include random mental images of undiscovered levels, most reports being 2 months before the level is discovered. Otherwise, the effects of macropsia (a condition that makes objects bigger than they are) may occur.   

Hyperesthesia (senses become amplified) may happen to random senses at random, if one experiences this constantly, or is exposed to it for a long time, they may experience overstimulation or exhibit symptoms of ADHD, eventually, these effects become long-term. Rarely, [[Object 1|Almond Water]] has shown to be a one-time cure or something to ease the effects. Potentially, pain may be amplified, however, few reports (4 to 9 each year) lead to this being so far unconfirmed.
==Entities==
===[[Hydrolitis Plague|Hydrolitis Varient RG]]===
While the plague is rare, being found exclusively in new rooms on the outskirts (water bottles are present, and this plague can be present in the water for 10 minutes to an hour); the plague on this level is a rare variant, consisting of symptoms such as congestion, calcium deficiency, random pain present in the arms, and rarely, migraines (some reports have shown this leading to sanity loss or delirium).
===Pneaxrious===
Pnexrious is a common bacterium that attempts to over-inflate the lungs. If one has weak hygiene, the bacteria will lead to lung collapse. However, this is extremely rare, with 46 cases reported throughout 2000 to 2020. However, it does cause symptoms in the process. Shortness of breath, severe coughing or sneezing, random lightheaded feelings. The bacteria disappear in 2 weeks to 1 month, [[Object 1|Almond Water]] shortening this.
===[[Entity 2|Windows]]===
Windows are present when one is on the verge of the insanity cycle. They also attract wanderers in a state of delirium or confusion. People who fall for it end up with a twisted ankle or fractured arm, and random splinter-like wounds present all around the body. One can encounter a window without going into the insanity cycle if they explore dark rooms long enough.
==Objects==
===Red Pill===
Each red pill contains electrolytes, enough to restore it to average levels (technical measurement is 135 mEq/L), along with large amounts of magnesium. The red pills' long-term effects have been reported to be slight energy deficiency at random times, especially during morning hours (6-8 am), and recklessness.
===Orange Pill===
Orange pills can cause V12 deficiency (V12 develops protection of nerve fibers, helps make neurotransmitters function better, repairs and does DNA synthesis, prevents brain atrophy; prevents Alzheimer's and dementia), randomly depleting for 2 to 5 days. It can also cause spatial awareness problems, motor skill problems, and some neurotransmitters failing to function. If one takes another pill while orange pills affect them, the orange pill effects will be cancelled out.
===Yellow Pill===
The yellow pill has been shown to cause random energy boosts, contributing to recklessness and restlessness. Random neurotransmitters are activated, except norepinephrine. People in this state have been found to have high amounts of caffeine in their bodies (1800mg). The yellow pill's effects clear up after a week to 2 weeks. It is recommended to not take 2 yellow pills at once, death being very common in these cases.
===Green Pill===
The green pill consists of high amounts of V6 (helps metabolism, helps with neurotransmitters function exclusively to serotonin, dopamine, and GABA, forms red blood cells, and helps the immune system), immediately affecting the body. You can only use this pill 2 times each level, attempting to do a 3rd time causes you to vomit the pill up. IVs have been shown to bypass this, however.
===Blue Pill===
Blue Pill is the deadliest pill of them all; this time affecting both entities and humans. The pill has the same effects as [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]], and [[Liquid Dread]]. However, in dead zone levels, it is found that this pill's effects are reversed, giving the same effects as the yellow pill, and red pill. If an entity (except for plagues) touches the blue pill, they start getting wounds. However, it is recommended to force-feed it to the entity to kill them instantly.
===Indigo Pill===
The indigo pill is theoretical; only being reported once in 2001. The reporter said that the pill has effects similar to Rose Water, a now-extinct water variant. It is not truly known if this exists, as the reporter most likely suffered from delirium. This is included due to the recent visions of the indigo pills when one is hallucinating or sleeping.
==Entrances and Exits==
===Entrances===
*Entering through a trapdoor with a sign on it saying “nostalgia” from [[Level 276]] will take the person who went in the trapdoor saying nostalgia here.
*Breaking a wall and finding another room with similarities to Level 257 from [[Level 192]] will take 3 to 40 minutes to teleport one into Level 257.
*Entering a room that looks like [[Level Fun]] on [[Level 274]] will make the room transform into Level 257.
*Hearing 90s music for more than 50 minutes on [[Level 245]] will suck a wanderer into the ground and teleport them here.
*Finding and taking a blue pill in [[Level 199]] will cause you to go unconscious and wake up in Level 257. It should be noted that if one is watching the person using this entry method, the teleportation will not work.
===Exits===
*Finding a white void randomly teleports the wanderer to [[Level 280]].
*When the people that are on the verge of dying, they get teleported into [[Level 278]]. (Cannot be done intentionally)
